steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank fills "______ compelling narrative still captivates readers today." The word must be a possessive pronoun referring to The Aeneid, which is a singular, non-person noun.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'their': plural possessive. The Aeneid is singular, so this creates a number mismatch. ✗
  • Choice B — 'they're': contraction of they are. Substituting gives "they are compelling narrative," which is not a possessive and is grammatically broken. ✗
  • Choice C — 'its': singular possessive for non-persons. Substituting gives "The Aeneid's compelling narrative still captivates readers today" — grammatically correct and meaning preserved. ✓
  • Choice D — 'it's': contraction of it is. Substituting gives "it is compelling narrative," which cannot serve as a possessive modifier. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
